title: Lichfield & Cannock Chase day out

main_route.excerpt: we have come out for the day to Lichfield & Cannock Chase
We drove on to have lunch at Cannock Chase

boundary_flags: mixed_end: diary page A_0140_R ends mid-sentence; return home assumed but not explicitly confirmed in available text
summary: A single-day family outing from Birmingham on Saturday 24 September 1994, visiting Lichfield Cathedral and the St. Mary's Heritage Centre before driving on to Cannock Chase for lunch and a walk on the common. No overnight stop; the party returned to Birmingham the same day.
extraction_notes: Page A_0140_R is a mixed_end boundary — the text cuts off mid-sentence ('Simon & Eve saw'). The rest of the day's account is not included in this bundle. Return home is assumed as the trip_id designates this a 'day-out'. No ferry crossings; entirely within England. The year 1994 is confirmed by the trip_id and the fact that Saturday September 24th falls on a Saturday in 1994. Travellers list is inferred from family context (all family members plus Tinder the dog); only Peter is specifically mentioned by name in the text ('Peter is more senior in the Cathedral Choir'). 'Cathedral Precinct', 'St. Mary's centre', and 'Walter Smith's' are sub-locations within Lichfield, not separate route stops.
